questions.text: What is the central theme of Surah Yunus?
questions.answer: The truth of Revelation, the Oneness of Allah, and the reality of the Messengers, with a focus on Allah's Wisdom and Power in creation.

questions.text: Why did people find it strange that Allah sent a human messenger?
questions.answer: They thought a messenger should be something supernatural, but Allah clarified that he is a man sent to warn and give glad tidings.

questions.text: How many days did Allah take to create the heavens and the earth as mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Six days.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the sun and the moon in the opening verses?
questions.answer: He made the sun a shining glory and the moon a derived light and determined for it phases so people could know the number of years and the count of time.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the alternation of the night and the day according to this Surah?
questions.answer: They are signs for a people who fear Allah (Muttaqin).

questions.text: What is the fate of those who do not expect to meet Allah and are satisfied with the life of this world?
questions.answer: Their refuge is the Fire because of what they used to earn.

questions.text: What will be the greeting and the final prayer of the people of Paradise?
questions.answer: Their greeting will be 'Salam' (Peace), and their final call will be 'Praise to Allah, Lord of the worlds.'

questions.text: How does man typically react when touched by hardship compared to when he is in ease?
questions.answer: During hardship, he calls upon Allah lying on his side, sitting, or standing; but when the hardship is removed, he continues as if he had never called upon Him.

questions.text: What was the reason for the destruction of previous generations mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: They did wrong and rejected the clear signs brought to them by their messengers.

questions.text: What was the demand of those who did not expect the meeting with Allah regarding the Quran?
questions.answer: They said, 'Bring us a Quran other than this or change it.'

questions.text: What was the Prophet's response to the demand to change the Quran?
questions.answer: He said it is not for him to change it of his own accord; he only follows what is revealed to him and fears the punishment of a Great Day.

questions.text: Did mankind start as one community or multiple religions?
questions.answer: Mankind was but one community (of one religion), then they differed.

questions.text: What do the polytheists say about their idols in terms of intercession?
questions.answer: They say, 'These are our intercessors with Allah,' but Allah informs them that He knows all that is in the heavens and the earth and has no such partners.

questions.text: What happens to people when they are on ships and a storm comes, and then when they are saved?
questions.answer: During the storm, they call upon Allah with sincere faith; but once saved, they again commit injustice and rebellion on earth.

questions.text: Describe the parable of the life of this world given in this Surah.
questions.answer: It is like rain that produces lush vegetation, which then suddenly becomes dry stubble as if it had not flourished the day before.

questions.text: What is 'Dar al-Salam'?
questions.answer: The Home of Peace (Paradise) to which Allah invites mankind.

questions.text: What is the reward for those who do good ('Al-Husna') in this Surah?
questions.answer: They will have the best reward (Paradise) and even more (the vision of Allah).

questions.text: How will the faces of the people of Paradise and the people of Hell appear?
questions.answer: The faces of the people of Paradise will have no gloom or humiliation, while the faces of the people of Hell will be covered as if by pieces of a dark night.

questions.text: What will the 'partners' (idols) say to their worshippers on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: They will deny that they were being worshipped and will say that Allah is a sufficient witness between them.

questions.text: What questions are the polytheists asked to prove Allah's Oneness regarding provision and life?
questions.answer: Who provides for you from the heaven and the earth? Who owns hearing and sight? Who brings the living out of the dead and the dead out of the living?

questions.text: Does 'assumption' (Zann) avail anything against the truth?
questions.answer: No, assumption avails nothing against the truth; indeed, Allah is Knowing of what they do.

questions.text: Could the Quran have been produced by anyone other than Allah?
questions.answer: No, it is a confirmation of what was before it and a detailed explanation of the Scripture—there is no doubt it is from the Lord of the worlds.

questions.text: What challenge does Allah give to those who say the Prophet fabricated the Quran?
questions.answer: To bring forth just one Surah like it and call upon anyone they can besides Allah for help.

questions.text: What is the reaction of some people to the truth that they cannot encompass with knowledge?
questions.answer: They deny it before its explanation has come to them.

questions.text: Is Allah unjust to mankind in any way?
questions.answer: No, 'Indeed, Allah does not wrong the people at all, but it is the people who are wronging themselves.'

questions.text: How long will the life of this world seem to people on the Day of Resurrection?
questions.answer: As if they had stayed only for an hour of a day.

questions.text: What will every soul wish to do if it has all the wealth on earth on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: It would try to ransom itself with it, but the judgment will be in justice.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the Quran's effect on the hearts?
questions.answer: As an admonition from your Lord, a healing for what is in the breasts, a guidance, and a mercy for the believers.

questions.text: What should the believers rejoice in more than worldly wealth?
questions.answer: In the bounty of Allah and His mercy (the Quran and Islam).

questions.text: What is the rebuke for those who invent lies about what Allah has made lawful or unlawful?
questions.answer: They are asked: 'Has Allah permitted you [to do so], or do you invent [something] about Allah?'

questions.text: Does anything on earth or in the heaven escape Allah's knowledge?
questions.answer: No, not even the weight of an atom escapes Him, nor anything smaller or larger.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the 'Awliya' (friends) of Allah?
questions.answer: They are those who believe and used to fear Allah (Taqwa); they shall have no fear, nor shall they grieve.

questions.text: What is the 'Boushra' (glad tidings) promised to the friends of Allah?
questions.answer: Glad tidings in the life of this world and in the Hereafter.

questions.text: Should the Prophet be grieved by the words and plots of the disbelievers?
questions.answer: No, for all honor and power belong to Allah, and He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

questions.text: What is the claim regarding Allah having a 'son' and how is it refuted?
questions.answer: Allah is Exalted! He is the Self-Sufficient. To Him belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth. Those who say this have no authority for it.

questions.text: Briefly describe the story of Nuh mentioned in this Surah.
questions.answer: Nuh challenged his people to carry out their plot against him if they could, while he relied on Allah. He was saved in the ship while the deniers were drowned.

questions.text: Who did Allah send to Fir'aun and his chiefs with signs?
questions.answer: Musa and Harun.

questions.text: What did Fir'aun's chiefs call the truth when it came to them?
questions.answer: They called it 'clear magic'.

questions.text: How many of the people of Musa believed in him initially due to fear of Fir'aun?
questions.answer: Only a few of his people (offspring/youths) believed, fearing that Fir'aun and his chiefs would persecute them.

questions.text: What advice did Musa give to his people to survive the persecution in Egypt?
questions.answer: To settle in houses, make their houses places of worship (Qiblah), and establish prayer.

questions.text: What was Musa's prayer regarding the wealth and power of Fir'aun?
questions.answer: He asked Allah to wipe out their wealth and harden their hearts so they would not believe until they saw the painful punishment.

questions.text: What did Fir'aun (Pharaoh) say when he was about to drown?
questions.answer: He said, 'I believe that there is no deity except He in whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am of the Muslims'.

questions.text: What was Allah's response to Pharaoh's last-minute conversion?
questions.answer: It was rejected because he had been disobedient and a corrupter until that very moment.

questions.text: Why did Allah preserve the body of Fir'aun?
questions.answer: To be a sign for those who come after him, though many of the people are heedless of Allah's signs.

questions.text: What happened to the Children of Israel after they were saved from Egypt?
questions.answer: They were settled in a beautiful settlement and provided with good things, yet they still differed after knowledge had come to them.

questions.text: Who should the Prophet ask if he were in doubt about what was revealed to him?
questions.answer: He is told to ask those who have been reading the Scripture before him (the sincere scholars of the People of the Book).

questions.text: What was the unique characteristic of the people of Prophet Yunus (AS)?
questions.answer: They were the only nation that believed as a whole when they saw the punishment coming, and Allah subsequently removed the punishment from them.

questions.text: Does Allah force people to become believers?
questions.answer: No; 'If your Lord had willed, those on earth would have believed—all of them entirely. Then, [O Muhammad], would you compel the people in order that they become believers?'

questions.text: What happens to those who do not use their reason (Aql)?
questions.answer: Allah places defilement (Rijs) upon them.

questions.text: What should the Prophet say if people remain in doubt about his religion?
questions.answer: He should say: 'I do not worship those you worship besides Allah, but I worship Allah who causes you to die.'

questions.text: What is the command regarding being a 'Hanif' (pure monotheist)?
questions.answer: The Prophet is commanded to direct his face toward the religion as a Hanif and never be among those who associate others with Allah.

questions.text: Can anyone remove a harm if Allah touches a person with it?
questions.answer: No, no one can remove it except Him.

questions.text: Can anyone withhold a good if Allah intends it for a person?
questions.answer: No, no one can repel His favor; He causes it to reach whom He wills of His servants.

questions.text: What is the final advice for the Prophet at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: To follow what is revealed to him and be patient until Allah gives judgment, for He is the best of judges.

questions.text: What is the significance of the title 'Yunus'?
questions.answer: It is named after Prophet Jonah, whose people set a unique example of collective repentance.

questions.text: What is the 'truth' mentioned that comes from the Lord?
questions.answer: The Quran and the message of Tawhid.

questions.text: How does Allah describe His 'Istawa' (establishment) in this Surah?
questions.answer: He established Himself above the Throne, conducting the affairs of the universe.

questions.text: What is the 'intercession' (Shafa'ah) rule mentioned here?
questions.answer: There is no intercessor except after His permission.

questions.text: What is the 'return' mentioned in the opening?
questions.answer: To Allah is the return of all; it is a true promise.

questions.text: How does Allah describe those who are ungrateful for His signs?
questions.answer: As being heedless (Ghafilun).

questions.text: What is the 'punishment of the burning' for?
questions.answer: For those who reject the signs of Allah after they have come to them.

questions.text: What is the 'darkness' a metaphor for in this Surah?
questions.answer: Misguidance and the state of those in Hell.

questions.text: What is the 'light' a metaphor for?
questions.answer: Guidance and the path of the believers.

questions.text: What is the 'shining glory' of the sun?
questions.answer: Dhiya (a source of light and heat).

questions.text: What is the 'derived light' of the moon?
questions.answer: Nur (a reflected light).

questions.text: Why does Allah mention the years and the count of time?
questions.answer: To show the order and purpose in His creation.

questions.text: What is the 'greatest success' (Al-Fawz al-'Azim)?
questions.answer: Attaining Paradise and Allah's pleasure.

questions.text: What is the 'painful punishment' for?
questions.answer: For those who were arrogant and rejected the truth.

questions.text: How does Allah view the 'inventing of lies'?
questions.answer: As the worst of sins.

questions.text: What is the 'witness' of Allah over all things?
questions.answer: His complete and perfect knowledge of every action.

questions.text: What is the 'admonition' (Maw'izah) of the Quran?
questions.answer: Its ability to warn and advise the souls.

questions.text: What is the 'healing' (Shifa') of the Quran?
questions.answer: Its ability to cure spiritual diseases like doubt, hypocrisy, and hatred.

questions.text: What is the 'mercy' (Rahmah) of the Quran?
questions.answer: The guidance and ease it provides to those who follow it.

questions.text: What is the 'bounty' (Fadl) of Allah?
questions.answer: His favors and gifts, primarily the revelation.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the 'plots' of the disbelievers?
questions.answer: As being weak against the plan of Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Home of Peace' calling people to?
questions.answer: A life of eternal security and happiness.

questions.text: What is the 'word of your Lord' that has been fulfilled?
questions.answer: His decree regarding the fate of the righteous and the wicked.

questions.text: What is the 'clear proof' (Bayyinah)?
questions.answer: Evidence that leaves no room for doubt.

questions.text: What is the 'patience' required of the Prophet?
questions.answer: Steadfastness in delivering the message despite opposition.

questions.text: What is the final message about the 'Lord of the Great Throne'?
questions.answer: That He is the ultimate authority and refuge.
